title: erigeron asperugineus
intro: Erigeron asperugineus, also known as the rough fleabane, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. This plant is native to North America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Erigeron asperugineus include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Erigeron asperugineus can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues.
